rows.key_dev: Fitch placed AT&T on Rating Watch Negative following Lumen fiber acquisition ($5.75B closed Feb 2026); net debt/EBITDA rising to ~3.2x post-deal.
Frontier acquisition closed Jan 20 2026; Q1 2026 beat with first positive Q1 postpaid phone net adds since 2013; raised 2026 EPS guidance to 5-6% growth.
Q1 2026 revenue +11% YoY to $31.46B driven by Olympics/Super Bowl 60; broadband subscriber losses improved YoY; Versant cable spin-off completed late 2025.
Q2 FY2026 adj. EPS $1.57 beat; targeting $8B+ share repurchases FY2026; streaming operating margin guided ~10%; ESPN NFL rights expansion announced.
WBD shareholders approved Paramount acquisition (~$110B deal, Apr 2026); S&P further downgraded post-merger to BB- with negative outlook; projected 7.6x leverage for 2026.
Netflix walked away from WBD studio/streaming acquisition (Feb 2026) after Paramount outbid; reconfirmed investment-grade A/A3 balance sheet with $12B+ FCF guided for 2026.
AWS AI infrastructure capex commitments accelerating in 2026; Amazon priced multi-billion debt issuance in late 2025 to fund cloud capex; Q1 2026 results beat estimates.
Alphabet issued ~$20B in debt in late 2025 for AI/GCP capex; Q1 2026 Cloud revenue growth strong; DOJ antitrust remedies proceedings ongoing in 2026.
Azure AI growth remains strong in Q1 2026; Copilot enterprise adoption accelerating; no material adverse credit developments in last 60 days.
Oracle announced plan to raise $45-50B gross in CY2026 via equity/debt/convertibles to fund OCI expansion; Moody's/S&P both on Negative outlook citing counterparty risk and rising leverage (~4x).
FY2026 record results: $41.5B revenue (+10% YoY), $72B RPO; $50B buyback authorization; Informatica acquisition integrated; $14.4B FCF.
IBM acquiring Confluent for ~$11B (closing mid-2026); Moody's outlook Negative since Apr 2024; deal funded with cash, raising leverage temporarily.
Apple Intelligence AI features expanding to more devices in 2026; DOJ antitrust proceedings ongoing; no material adverse credit developments in last 60 days.
Q2 FY2026 EPS $0.56 beat by 70%; Q3 FY2026 earnings expected Jun 3 2026, within 14 days; ACV billings growth solid with strong cloud momentum.
All three agencies carrying Positive outlook on Broadcom; VMware integration progressing; AI ASIC chip demand accelerating; Q1 FY2026 beat estimates.
HP Inc Q2 FY2026 PC and printing segment results in line; AI PC refresh cycle beginning to lift commercial demand; no material adverse developments.
HPE selected Ingram Micro and TD SYNNEX as global distribution partners (May 2026); Juniper Networks integration advancing; Q2 FY2026 earnings due Jun 3 2026 within 14 days.
Dell AI server backlog growing strongly driven by hyperscaler GPU server demand; PowerEdge infrastructure business accelerating; no material adverse credit events.
Q4 FY2026 earnings expected ~May 27 2026 within 7 days; solar tracker demand strong on IRA tailwinds; monitoring tariff policy impact on utility-scale solar projects.
Board authorized new $600M share repurchase program (Apr 27 2026); Q2 FY2026 revenue $4.01B with solid non-GAAP margin of 6.4%; strong cash from operations of $399M.
Announced spin-off of data center EMS business (~$6.6Bn revenue) and acquisition of Electrical Power Products. S&P affirmed BBB-/Stable May 6 2026; pro forma leverage expected to remain below 3x.
Jabil Q2 FY2026 revenue $8.3B ahead of expectations; raised FY2026 outlook; FCF guidance $1.3B+; data center and AI infrastructure driving outperformance.
Arrow launched Digital Test Drive cloud-based platform (May 5 2026); components demand stabilizing after inventory correction; no material adverse credit events.
Avnet electronic component distribution stabilizing; AI/embedded computing segment demand improving; FY2026 Q4 results expected Aug 2026.
TD SYNNEX named HPE global distribution partner (May 2026); completed Exclusive Networks AV/UC/gaming division acquisition Jan 2026; Q3 FY2026 results due ~Jul 1 2026.
Ingram Micro named HPE global distribution partner (May 2026); recently re-listed on NYSE (Oct 2024); AI infrastructure and cloud services distribution growing.
CDW Q1 2026 results reported; IT solutions demand steady with enterprise AI workload spending lifting hardware/services. No material adverse credit events.
S&P placed Kyndryl BBB- on CreditWatch (Feb 10 2026); company continuing IT services transformation with improving margins; alliances with hyperscalers expanding.
Cognizant Q1 2026 revenue growth improving; AI services adoption accelerating among enterprise clients; no material adverse credit developments in last 60 days.
Moody's upgraded Equinix to Baa1 (Mar 5 2026) citing strong digital infrastructure demand, geographic scale, and improved credit metrics; FY2026 revenue guided $10.1-10.2B.
Digital Realty FY2025 revenue $6.1B; FY2026 guided $6.6-6.7B; record leasing activity and strong AI-driven data center demand backlog providing visibility.
AMT Q1 2026 AFFO attributable to common stockholders +2.6% to $1.32B; data center segment revenue guided +12.5%; Moody's outlook Positive reflecting improving credit metrics.
Q1 2026 EPS beat but stock fell ~9% on margin compression; new CEO Enrique Lores sharpening strategy; Q2 2026 non-GAAP EPS guided ~-9% YoY; FCF guidance at least $6B.
Corpay Q1 2026 revenue +25% to $1.26B, adj. EPS +29%; 11% organic revenue growth for 4th consecutive quarter; increased full year revenue guidance to $5.25-5.33B.
Mastercard Q1 2026 net revenue and volume growth strong; cross-border volumes continue to expand; no material adverse credit developments in last 60 days.
Visa Q2 FY2026 payments volume and cross-border transactions growing; DOJ antitrust case on debit network practices ongoing; no material adverse credit changes.
Lagardère FY2025 earnings call held Feb 19 2026; Travel Retail and Publishing segments performing in line; Vivendi majority ownership continues; H1 2026 results expected Sep 2026.
Q1 2026 EPS of $1.14 beat estimates; full-year 2026 guidance raised. Q2 next earnings Aug 5, 2026.
Q1 2026 gross bookings +25% YoY to $53.7B; CFO change effective Feb 2026 as Balaji Krishnamurthy replaced Prashanth Mahendra-Rajah. Record $3.0B buyback in Q1.
Q1 2026 EPS beat at $0.64; but guided Q2 pre-tax profit ~$1B on $2B+ fuel spike from Middle East tensions. Fuel cost surge flagged as key near-term risk.
Record Q1 2026 results; launched $2.5B buyback and PROPEL targets through 2029. Completed DLC unification on May 7, 2026, canceling LSE listing. Fuel headwind flagged.
Q1 2026 adj. EPS $3.60 beat; FY2026 adj. EPS guidance $17.10–$17.50. Returned $1.1B to shareholders via buybacks and dividends in Q1. Fuel cost headwind flagged.
May 4 earnings: full-year 2026 guidance cut; net yield projected -3% to -5%; adj. EPS slashed to $1.45–$1.79. Geopolitical headwinds hit Europe bookings. Stock -9% on day.
Middle East conflict and elevated fuel prices weigh on Turkey-based carrier. No material rating actions in last 60 days.
All four credit ratings unchanged as of Apr 21, 2026 per 10-Q filing. Q1 2026 results showed Ford Pro strength; GM guided strong truck/SUV performance in 2026.
Q1 2026 deliveries 358K, revenue $22.4B (+16% YoY); adj. EBITDA margin 16.4%. Ending Model S/X sales to repurpose Fremont for Optimus robots. Cybercab production start targeted 2026.
S&P maintains BBB- with Negative outlook (Feb 2025); margin recovery below 8% threshold. Q1 2026 Ford Blue EBIT $1.9B; Model e losses persist at -$777M. Novelis disruption flagged.
No material news. FY2026 guidance maintains steady production outlook; tariff impact from US policy being evaluated.
Ongoing restructuring with plant closures and headcount reductions. Honda merger talks collapsed. FY2025 loss reported; strategic review underway amid deepening China volume losses.
No material news. Q1 2026 results showed resilient premium demand; US tariff impact being absorbed. Recall-related warranty costs moderated.
Both Moody's and S&P outlooks Negative amid China volume loss, restructuring costs, and EV margin pressure. Ongoing workforce restructuring at VW brand and Audi.
No material news. Luxury demand resilient; Q1 2026 showed stable margins despite China volume pressure and EV cost headwinds.
All three agencies rate at A-level as of 2024. US tariff exposure a risk but strong North America mix mitigates. No material news in last 60 days.
Moody's affirmed Baa3/Stable Dec 2025 post Spirit AeroSystems acquisition; Fitch revised outlook to Stable Jun 2025. 737 at 42/month; FCF turned positive in Q1 2026.
Q1 2026 EPS $1.86 beat; revenue $11.6B +29% YoY; orders +87% driven by Commercial Engine Services. FY2026 guidance maintained near high end. Record backlog ~$190B.
No material news. Q1 2026 truck market softness in EU offset by resilient North America; electric truck rollout on track.
No material news. Q1 2026 truck deliveries slightly below prior year on softening North America Class 8 market; financial services performing well.
No material news. Atmus filtration spin-off fully completed; Accelera electrification segment ramping; Q1 2026 revenue in line with prior year.
Q1 2026 sales +13% YoY to $2.95B; adj. EPS +18.9% to $2.71; backlog $30.8B. Full-year EPS guidance raised. Strong freight and transit segment performance.
Agriculture equipment demand soft globally; Q1 2026 saw volume declines in core farm machinery. Reviewing strategic options including potential business separations.
FY2026 Q4 results strong; e-commerce and advertising revenue growth accelerating. Tariff risk hedged via global supply chain diversification. No material negative news.
No material news. Domestic same-store sales growth positive; DIFM commercial business expanding. Share buyback program ongoing.
No material news. Auto and industrial parts demand stable. European operations facing modest headwinds from soft industrial market.
No material news. Membership renewal rates at record highs; Q3 2026 comps tracking above consensus expectations.
No material news. Pro contractor business resilient; housing market recovery supporting DIY demand. SRS Distribution integration progressing on track.
No material news. Q1 2026 same-store sales growth positive; DIFM segment outperforming. Canada expansion progressing well.
No material news. Travel retail business benefiting from airport passenger recovery; UK High Street segment stable after recent disposals.

No material news. Q1 2026 organic revenue growth solid; price/mix favorable. Volume growth positive across key markets including EM.
No material news. Solid execution as largest Coke bottler in US. Q1 2026 revenue and margins in line with expectations.

IQOS smoke-free product volumes growing strongly; ZYN nicotine pouches gaining US market share. Q1 2026 results showed accelerating growth in heated tobacco.
Q1 2026 solid results; declared $1.06/share quarterly dividend. on! nicotine pouch volume growing; Marlboro cigarette share stable. Stock +14.5% over past month.
No material news. NGP (next-gen products) gaining traction in key European markets; debt reduction on track. FY2026 interim results expected in May.
No material news. Leaf tobacco supply/demand in balance; plant-based ingredients segment growing. Dividend maintained at $0.82/share quarterly.

Q1 2026 earnings beat (EPS $1.97 vs. $1.93 est.); Q2 2026 next report Jul 31. KMB acquired Kenvue consumer brands assets; expects margin recovery H2 2026.
Q1 2026 EPS of -$0.56 badly missed $0.62 est.; revenue fell to $3.27B vs. $3.51B exp.; stock near 52-week low; sub-IG rated Ba1/BB+/BB+ with Negative outlook at Moody's/Fitch.
Q1 2026 EPS of $0.67 beat by 9.8%; revenue $10.08B exceeded est. by 3.4%. Company reaffirmed full-year 2026 EPS guidance; cocoa cost tailwinds expected to benefit 2026 margins.

Q1 2026 adj. EPS +11% YoY to $1.86; record adj. EBITDA ~$2.1B in 2025; net leverage reduced to 2.5x. Announced new greenfield beverage can plant in Northern India; 35% dividend increase.

FDA review of Keytruda + Padcev combination in MIBC expected by Aug 17, 2026; Merck projecting 1.9% revenue increase in 2026 amid patent expiry headwinds on Keytruda in late 2020s.

Pfizer faces LOE headwinds of $3B+ in 2027 and $6B+ in 2028; continued revenue trajectory decline from COVID product wind-down. Non-COVID operational revenues stabilizing via Vyndaqel, Eliquis, and Seagen oncology portfolio.

Q2 FY2026 revenue $78.4B (+3.8%); adj. EPS $4.46 +8% YoY. Full acquisition of OneOncology completed; $1.1B remeasurement gain recorded. Core U.S. growth ~7% ex-specific customer losses.

Q1 2026 occupancy 98.9%, Net Debt/Annualized Adj. EBITDAre 5.2x; AFFO/share guidance $4.41–$4.44 for FY2026. Portfolio of 15,571 properties across 1,786 clients. No adverse credit events.

Phillips 66 Q1 2026 adjusted earnings $200M, down sharply from $1B in Q4 2025; $839M mark-to-market losses from derivative hedges; dividend raised 7%; liquidity ~$6B.
Scarborough Energy Project 96% complete, targeting first LNG Q4 2026; Q1 2026 revenue $3.26B +7% QoQ; CEO change underway with efficiency review; liquidity $8.3B as of Mar 31, 2026.
Equinor holds Aa2/AA- ratings with sovereign uplift (Norwegian state 67% owner); RCF extended to Nov 2030; Q1 2026 results reported Apr 2026 with stable production guidance maintained.
Shell reported solid Q1 2026 results; Q1 LNG liquefaction volumes steady; continues $3.5B/quarter buyback program; no material adverse credit events in last 60 days.
TotalEnergies gearing at ~17% through Q3 2025; declared third interim dividend +7.6% for FY2025; hydrocarbon production growth above 4% YoY; Q1 2026 results reported Apr 25, 2026.
Halliburton Q1 2026 revenue $5.4B flat YoY; operating income rose to $679M driven by international recovery; Middle East disruptions noted; North America showing early-innings recovery signs per CEO.
SLB raised quarterly dividend 3.5% to $0.295/share; committed $4B+ shareholder returns for 2026; Q1 2026 results in line; strong digital and international segment performance.
Baker Hughes Q1 2026 net income $930M (+6% YoY); IET backlog record $32.4B; 1 GW power deal for North America signed; Google Cloud AI partnership announced for data center optimization.
TechnipFMC Q1 2026 results strong; major subsea contract wins with Petrobras (risers/flowlines) and YPF Argentina (Vaca Muerta); subsea backlog growing on offshore E&P spending cycle.
Cheniere reported solid Q1 2026; Sabine Pass and Corpus Christi operating near full capacity; strong LNG export demand underpinned by long-term SPAs; no material adverse credit events.
Enbridge Q1 2026 DCF in line; Line 3 and US gas distribution assets performing; US gas utilities integration (Dominion Gas assets acquired 2024) proceeding on schedule.

NextEra announced $66.8B all-stock acquisition of Dominion Energy on May 18, 2026, creating world's largest regulated utility; deal requires multi-state/federal regulatory approval; 12–18 months to close.
NextEra Energy Partners monitors implications of parent NEE's $66.8B Dominion acquisition announced May 18; LP unit distribution trajectory and IDR structure under scrutiny for any restructuring.
Duke Energy Q1 2026 results solid; large-load data center demand driving 5-year capex plan of ~$75B; Carolinas rate case filings progressing; no material adverse credit events in last 60 days.
Sempra Q1 2026 results in line; Port Arthur LNG Phase 1 construction progressing; SoCalGas and SDG&E regulated utility operations stable; no material adverse credit events in last 60 days.

AES Corporation continues transition to renewables; Q1 2026 results in line; data center PPA pipeline growing; leverage remains elevated vs. investment-grade peers; S&P outlook Positive on debt reduction path.

Dominion Energy agreed to be acquired by NextEra Energy in $66.8B all-stock deal announced May 18, 2026; shareholders to receive 0.8138 NEE shares each; pending multi-state and FERC regulatory approvals.
Edison International faces ongoing wildfire liability exposure from LA wildfires (Jan 2025); multiple agency Negative outlooks reflecting uncertainty over insurance recovery and rate base treatment.

Constellation Energy Q1 2026 results strong; nuclear fleet performing at high capacity factors; Calpine acquisition completed; growing AI/data center power PPAs underpinning long-term revenue visibility.

Southern Company Vogtle Unit 4 in commercial operation; Georgia Power rate case outcome supportive; strong load growth from data centers and manufacturing in Southeast; no material adverse credit events.
PPL Q1 2026 EPS $0.63 (+5% YoY); reaffirmed 2026 EPS guidance $1.90–1.98; $5.1B capex plan for 2026; 6–8% annual EPS growth target through at least 2029 reaffirmed.

NRG Energy Q1 2026 in line; VitraChem/Vivint Smart Home integrations generating synergies; data center PPA opportunities accelerating; S&P/Fitch Positive outlooks signal potential IG upgrade trajectory.

RWE Q1 2026 results in line; accelerating offshore wind and battery storage investments across Europe and US; German nuclear phase-out fully absorbed; no material adverse credit events in last 60 days.
Uniper H1 2025 adjusted EBITDA fell 78% to €379M on lower generation and trading activity; German government ownership stake reducing; capacity market revenues key to near-term earnings recovery.

Engie Q1 2026 results in line; large-scale renewables and LNG portfolio performing well; French government partial ownership provides implicit support; no material adverse credit events in last 60 days.
Enel Q1 2026 in line; accelerating asset disposals and debt reduction on track; Italian state (MEF) ~23% shareholder provides credit support; renewables capacity additions progressing globally.
Clearway Energy Q1 2026 CAFD generation in line; contracted wind/solar fleet performing with strong capacity factors; TotalEnergies parent support; no material adverse credit events in last 60 days.
Brookfield Renewable record Q1 2026 FFO $1,394M LTM (+12% YoY); completed Boralex acquisition; ~$2.8B asset recycling proceeds; advancing AP1000 nuclear projects with US government; BBB+ S&P rating.
Fluence Energy FY2025 results showed revenue growth but continued FCF pressure; BESS backlog expanding on AI/grid demand; AES and Siemens Energy parent support; not publicly rated by major agencies.

Amrize (NYSE: AMRZ) spun off from Holcim Jun 23, 2025; $11.7B revenue; rated BBB+/Baa1 at inception; North America's largest cement supplier; benefiting from US infrastructure and data center build-out demand.

CEMEX Q1 2026 volumes impacted by US tariff uncertainty; management executing on leverage reduction toward IG territory; US operations face competition from Amrize post-Holcim spinoff; MXN/USD exposure persists.
